action.hypothesis: The EF=32 `factorized_topk` variant improved with more capacity, but lowering LR regressed sharply, so the family now looks more sensitive to support-shaping regularization than to further optimizer slowdown. The cleanest remaining single-variable stabilization check is to raise `AUXK_ALPHA` on the best factorized recipe (EF=32, LR=1.6e-3) and test whether stronger dead-feature recovery improves utilization enough to recover proxy FVU.
action.summary: Run a `factorized_topk` proxy stabilization pass at `EXPANSION_FACTOR=32`, `K=128`, `LR=1.6e-3`, and `AUXK_ALPHA=0.03125` to test whether stronger aux-k regularization improves the best current factorized setup.
action.self_review: This stays in exploitation mode on the only active incubating family, changes exactly one variable, and follows the remaining obvious stabilization axis after the LR regression. The main risk is that aux-k also fails to help, but that outcome would be useful evidence that the family is near exhaustion and ready to archive.
notes_to_memory: `factorized_topk` at EF=32 improved over EF=16, but reducing LR from 1.6e-3 to 8e-4 regressed from proxy FVU 0.3188326023519039 to 0.3457685150206089, so lower LR is not the right stabilization direction.
The best current factorized recipe remains EF=32, LR=1.6e-3, AUXK_ALPHA=0.01 at K=128.
This round tests the remaining obvious non-architectural stabilizer on that best recipe: stronger aux-k pressure for feature recovery and usage balance.
next_hypotheses: If higher AUXK improves `factorized_topk`, continue with one final nearby AUXK check or consider promotion pressure only if the gain is substantial.
If higher AUXK does not help, archive `factorized_topk` as exhausted under the current execution layer and return to a different search direction.
If AUXK improves quality but hurts runtime or stability, consider a small code/performance follow-up only if the quality gain is meaningful.
